+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KQ7GWE9V2XKWYAQ0HCPDE0EZ
+- 01KQ7K7KZNR3EHS9SY0XY79NYE
+- 01KQ5PSMYE3Q60SV8270S6K819
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: a980
+project: spatial-nav
+title: End-to-end spatial-nav smoke test — mount full <App/> and walk every gesture
+---
+## What
+
+The release-blocker card `01KQ5PEHWTEVTKPS2JHSZTXNBE` was supposed to gate "spatial-nav focus is not actually visible to users" with a per-component verification protocol. It was marked `done`, **yet the user has subsequently reported four production regressions** that the per-component test suite did not catch:
+
+1. Double-click on a perspective tab opened the inspector — `01KQ7GM77B1E6YH8Z893K05VKY`.
+2. Enter on a focused perspective tab did nothing instead of starting rename — `01KQ7GE3KY91X2YR6BX5AY40VK`.
+3. Nav.right from a card was trapped inside the column — `01KQ7GWE9V2XKWYAQ0HCPDE0EZ`.
+4. The implementer slipped in an unauthorized "ring" focus-indicator variant — `01KQ7G7SCN7ZQD4TFGP5EH4FFX`.
+
+The pattern: every per-component test renders one component with **hand-rolled providers** and stubbed-shape contexts. None of them mounts the production `<App/>` and walks gestures across the real composition. So bugs that live in the seams between components — the dblclick handler bubbling from a button through a chrome-monikered `<FocusScope>`, the keymap dispatch flowing past a perspective tab without a scope binding, the registration shape that diverges between `<BoardView>` in isolation vs. `<BoardView>` inside the real provider stack — go undetected.
+
+The closest existing prior art is `kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-view.cross-column-nav.spatial.test.tsx` (1,300 lines, in flight under `01KQ7GWE9V2XKWYAQ0HCPDE0EZ`). It mounts `<BoardView>` inside production providers, captures every `spatial_register_*` invoke into a JS shadow registry, and ports `BeamNavStrategy` from `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/navigate.rs` so `spatial_navigate` invocations are answered by the real algorithm against the captured graph. **That pattern is correct.** This card commissions its sibling: the same pattern applied to the **full `<App/>`** with the **complete gesture set**.
+
+### Why the per-component cards' "integration tests" missed this
+
+Two failure modes:
+
+- **Stubbed data contexts.** `useBoardData` / `usePerspectives` / `useViews` / `useUIState` are stubbed to return synthetic shapes. The real path goes `RustEngineContainer` → store → hooks. Bugs that live in the data-flow / provider-ordering wiring don't reproduce in the stubbed harness.
+- **Component-shaped registry, not app-shaped registry.** A `<BoardView>` mounted in isolation registers `ui:board → column:A → task:1A` cleanly. The same `<BoardView>` mounted inside `<App/>` also registers `nav:bar`, `ui:perspective-bar`, `ui:view`, `inspector:*`, etc. Cross-zone beam-search candidate selection depends on the **full** layer's leaf set; isolated tests can't see candidates that only exist when the rest of the app is mounted.
+
+The fix: a single test file that mounts `<App/>` and exercises every gesture — and which fails when any of these four regressions sneak back in.
+
+### The shape of the test
+
+#### File location
+
+`kanban-app/ui/src/spatial-nav-end-to-end.spatial.test.tsx` (new file, top of `src/` because it is app-level, not component-level).
+
+#### Mount the full App
+
+```tsx
+import App from "@/App";
+
+await render(<App />);
+```
+
+`<App/>` already composes `SpatialFocusProvider → FocusLayer → CommandBusyProvider → RustEngineContainer → WindowContainer → AppModeContainer → BoardContainer → NavBar / ViewsContainer / PerspectivesContainer / PerspectiveContainer / ViewContainer / InspectorsContainer`. The test stubs no providers — it stubs only the **Tauri boundary** (`@tauri-apps/api/core` invoke, `@tauri-apps/api/event` listen, `@tauri-apps/api/window` getCurrent / WebviewWindow) so production data flows through `RustEngineContainer` from a fixture.
+
+#### Tauri boundary stub
+
+Three invokes need real responses for `<App/>` to bootstrap:
+
+- `kanban_state_snapshot` (or whatever `RustEngineContainer` calls on mount) → returns the fixture (a board with 3 columns × 3 cards each, 2 perspectives, schema with `task.title`, `task.status`, `column.name`, `board.percent_complete`).
+- `ui_state_snapshot` → returns `{ keymap_mode: "cua", windows: { main: { palette_open: false, palette_mode: "command", inspector_stack: [] } } }`.
+- Any other bootstrap call the production code makes during `useEffect` mount — discover by booting the test against `mockInvoke = vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined)`, capturing the unhandled command names from `mockInvoke.mock.calls`, and adding a fixture response for each.
+
+The remaining invokes — `spatial_register_zone`, `spatial_register_focusable`, `spatial_register_layer`, `spatial_focus`, `spatial_navigate`, `spatial_drill_in`, `spatial_drill_out`, `spatial_unregister_scope`, `spatial_update_rect` — are routed through the same shadow-registry + ported-navigator harness as `board-view.cross-column-nav.spatial.test.tsx`. **Reuse that harness** — extract its `vi.hoisted` setup and the ported `BeamNavStrategy` into a shared helper:
+
+`kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-shadow-registry.ts` (new) — exports `setupSpatialHarness()` that returns `{ mockInvoke, fireFocusChanged, registry, getRegisteredKeyByMoniker(m) }`. Both the cross-column-nav test and this new end-to-end test import from it. No duplication.
+
+#### Fixture
+
+`kanban-app/ui/src/test/fixtures/end-to-end-board.ts` (new):
+
+- Board `board:E2E` with `name = "End-to-End Test Board"`, `percent_complete = 50`.
+- 3 columns: `column:TODO`, `column:DOING`, `column:DONE`.
+- Each column has 3 task cards (`task:T1..T3`, `task:D1..D3`, `task:N1..N3`) with unique titles.
+- 2 perspectives: `perspective:default` (active) and `perspective:secondary`, both with `view: "board"`.
+- Schema with at least `task.title`, `task.status`, `column.name`, `board.percent_complete` field defs.
+
+#### Gestures to walk
+
+The test file contains one `describe` block per gesture family. Each test renders the app once at the top of the block (or per test if the gesture mutates state), drives the gesture, and asserts on observable DOM **and** the `mockInvoke` call log.
+
+**Family 1 — Click → focus indicator visible.** For each of: a card, a column body whitespace, a perspective tab, a nav-bar button, a field row in the inspector:
+- Click the element matched by `[data-moniker="<expected>"]`.
+- Assert exactly one `mockInvoke("spatial_focus", { key })` call with the correct registered key (look up via `getRegisteredKeyByMoniker`).
+- After `fireFocusChanged(key)`, assert the element carries `data-focused="true"` AND its descendant `[data-testid="focus-indicator"]` is mounted with the bar class signature (`pointer-events-none absolute -left-2 …`).
+- Critically: assert exactly ONE `[data-focused="true"]` element exists in the document at any time — locks in the "single focus claim" invariant.
+
+**Family 2 — hjkl / arrow navigation within and across zones.**
+- Focus `task:T1`. Press `j` (vim) / `ArrowDown` (cua). Lands on `task:T2`.
+- Press `j` again → `task:T3`.
+- Press `l` / `ArrowRight` → lands on a card in `column:DOING` (the cross-column regression).
+- Press `l` again → lands on a card in `column:DONE`.
+- Press `h` / `ArrowLeft` → lands back in `column:DOING`.
+- All four directions cycle correctly; no direction silently no-ops when a candidate exists.
+
+**Family 3 — Drill in / Drill out.**
+- Focus a column, press Enter → `mockInvoke("spatial_drill_in", { key: <column-key> })`. Subsequent `fireFocusChanged` drives focus to the column's first child leaf (`<column.name>` header or `task:T1`).
+- Focus a card, press Escape → `mockInvoke("spatial_drill_out", …)`. Focus lands on the card's parent column.
+
+**Family 4 — Space → inspect.**
+- Focus a card, press Space. Asserts `mockInvoke` was called for `ui.inspect` with `target: "task:T1"`. Asserts the inspector mounts (the inspector container shows the task entity).
+- Focus a perspective tab, press Space. Asserts NO `ui.inspect` dispatch — perspective is not an entity.
+
+**Family 5 — Enter → rename on perspective tab.**
+- Focus the active perspective tab, press Enter. Asserts `<InlineRenameEditor>` mounts (CodeMirror `.cm-editor` is a descendant of `[data-moniker^="perspective_tab:"]`).
+- Type "RenameTest", press Enter inside the editor. Asserts `mockInvoke("perspective.rename", { args: { id, new_name: <original> + "RenameTest" } })`.
+- Locks in `01KQ7GE3KY91X2YR6BX5AY40VK`.
+
+**Family 6 — dblclick policy.**
+- Double-click a card. Asserts `mockInvoke` for `ui.inspect` with `target: "task:T1"`.
+- Double-click a perspective tab. Asserts `<InlineRenameEditor>` mounts AND zero `ui.inspect` calls. Locks in `01KQ7GM77B1E6YH8Z893K05VKY`.
+- Double-click a nav-bar button background, the perspective bar background, the view container chrome. Each one: zero `ui.inspect` calls.
+
+**Family 7 — Single focus indicator, no ring variant.**
+- After mounting and focusing each leaf type, walk the document. Every `[data-testid="focus-indicator"]` carries the bar class signature; none carries `inset-0` or `ring-2`. Locks in `01KQ7G7SCN7ZQD4TFGP5EH4FFX`.
+
+**Family 8 — Registry shape audit.** Walk the captured `mockInvoke` calls for `spatial_register_*`. Assert:
+- `task:*` registered as `spatial_register_focusable` (leaves), never as `spatial_register_zone`.
+- Each `task:*` carries `parent_zone === <its column's zone key>`.
+- Each `column:*` carries `parent_zone === <ui:board's zone key>`.
+- One `layer_key` per window. (If quick-capture pops a second window in the test, allow two; otherwise one.)
+- Every `<Inspectable>`-wrapped entity (or `inspectOnDoubleClick={true}` if the wrapper refactor `01KQ7K7KZNR3EHS9SY0XY79NYE` hasn't landed yet) was the only path to `useDispatchCommand("ui.inspect")`.
+
+**Family 9 — Edit-mode keystroke containment.** Focus a text input (e.g. inline rename editor or a card title editor). Type "abc". Assert ZERO `mockInvoke("spatial_navigate", …)` calls during the keystrokes — the input owns them. Locks in the editor-keystroke-isolation contract.
+
+### Why this card depends on three others
+
+- `01KQ7GWE9V2XKWYAQ0HCPDE0EZ` — until cross-column nav works, family 2 cannot pass.
+- `01KQ7K7KZNR3EHS9SY0XY79NYE` — the `<Inspectable>` refactor changes which file owns the `ui.inspect` dispatch. Family 6's "no inspect dispatch on chrome" assertion is identical either way (boolean opt-in or wrapper composition), but Family 8's "single dispatch site" audit only fires post-refactor.
+- `01KQ5PSMYE3Q60SV8270S6K819` — once `<Focusable>` is deleted, the registry-shape audit can drop the legacy primitive's name from its allowlist, simplifying Family 8.
+
+If any of those three are still in flight when this card starts, the implementer either rebases or files small follow-ups; do not start this card before they're done.
+
+### What this card does NOT do
+
+- Does not introduce new app behavior. It only adds a test file and shared harness.
+- Does not refactor the existing per-component spatial tests. They keep their narrow scope; this test is the umbrella.
+- Does not run on every CI invocation as a fast unit — by design it is a slow browser-mode integration test. Add it to the existing `vitest run` browser project so it runs on PRs to `kanban-app/ui/`.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/spatial-nav-end-to-end.spatial.test.tsx` exists and contains all nine gesture families above.
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-shadow-registry.ts` exports `setupSpatialHarness()` and is consumed by both this test and `board-view.cross-column-nav.spatial.test.tsx` (existing test refactored to import from the helper, no duplication).
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/test/fixtures/end-to-end-board.ts` exists with the 3×3 board fixture, 2 perspectives, and minimal schema.
+- [ ] The test mounts `<App/>` directly — not a partial component. Greppable: `import App from "@/App"` appears in the file.
+- [ ] All nine families pass. Removing the fix from any of `01KQ7GM77B1E6YH8Z893K05VKY`, `01KQ7GE3KY91X2YR6BX5AY40VK`, `01KQ7GWE9V2XKWYAQ0HCPDE0EZ`, or `01KQ7G7SCN7ZQD4TFGP5EH4FFX` re-introduces the regression and a specific family fails — verified by `git revert <fix-commit>` locally and re-running.
+- [ ] `cd kanban-app/ui && npm test` runs the file as part of the browser project (no separate test command, no opt-in flag).
+- [ ] CI workflow `.github/workflows/*.yml` runs the test on every PR that touches `kanban-app/ui/`.
+
+## Tests
+
+This card IS the test. Its own pass criterion is that the new file's nine families all run green against the post-prerequisites tree. No additional test files are required.
+
+### How to run
+
+```
+cd kanban-app/ui && npm test -- spatial-nav-end-to-end
+```
+
+(Or unfiltered: `cd kanban-app/ui && npm test`.) Headless on CI.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd`. Order:
+  1. Extract `setupSpatialHarness()` from the existing `board-view.cross-column-nav.spatial.test.tsx` into the shared helper — confirm that test still passes.
+  2. Build the fixture file.
+  3. Write the failing Family 1 (click → focus indicator) — confirms the harness mounts `<App/>` correctly.
+  4. Add families 2–9 one at a time, each one green before moving on.
+  5. Run `git revert` on each of the four prerequisite-fix commits in turn (locally, do not push) and confirm the matching family fails. Restore.
+- The test file will be larger than the project's normal sizing limit (target 800–1500 lines). That is acceptable for an end-to-end harness — the alternative is sprinkling the same setup across nine files.

changes.value: End-to-end spatial-nav smoke test — mount full <App/> and walk every gesture

## What

The release-blocker card `01KQ5PEHWTEVTKPS2JHSZTXNBE` was supposed to gate "spatial-nav focus is not actually visible to users" with a per-component verification protocol. It was marked `done`, **yet the user has subsequently reported four production regressions** that the per-component test suite did not catch:

1. Double-click on a perspective tab opened the inspector — `01KQ7GM77B1E6YH8Z893K05VKY`.
2. Enter on a focused perspective tab did nothing instead of starting rename — `01KQ7GE3KY91X2YR6BX5AY40VK`.
3. Nav.right from a card was trapped inside the column — `01KQ7GWE9V2XKWYAQ0HCPDE0EZ`.
4. The implementer slipped in an unauthorized "ring" focus-indicator variant — `01KQ7G7SCN7ZQD4TFGP5EH4FFX`.

The pattern: every per-component test renders one component with **hand-rolled providers** and stubbed-shape contexts. None of them mounts the production `<App/>` and walks gestures across the real composition. So bugs that live in the seams between components — the dblclick handler bubbling from a button through a chrome-monikered `<FocusScope>`, the keymap dispatch flowing past a perspective tab without a scope binding, the registration shape that diverges between `<BoardView>` in isolation vs. `<BoardView>` inside the real provider stack — go undetected.

The closest existing prior art is `kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-view.cross-column-nav.spatial.test.tsx` (1,300 lines, in flight under `01KQ7GWE9V2XKWYAQ0HCPDE0EZ`). It mounts `<BoardView>` inside production providers, captures every `spatial_register_*` invoke into a JS shadow registry, and ports `BeamNavStrategy` from `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/navigate.rs` so `spatial_navigate` invocations are answered by the real algorithm against the captured graph. **That pattern is correct.** This card commissions its sibling: the same pattern applied to the **full `<App/>`** with the **complete gesture set**.

### Why the per-component cards' "integration tests" missed this

Two failure modes:

- **Stubbed data contexts.** `useBoardData` / `usePerspectives` / `useViews` / `useUIState` are stubbed to return synthetic shapes. The real path goes `RustEngineContainer` → store → hooks. Bugs that live in the data-flow / provider-ordering wiring don't reproduce in the stubbed harness.
- **Component-shaped registry, not app-shaped registry.** A `<BoardView>` mounted in isolation registers `ui:board → column:A → task:1A` cleanly. The same `<BoardView>` mounted inside `<App/>` also registers `nav:bar`, `ui:perspective-bar`, `ui:view`, `inspector:*`, etc. Cross-zone beam-search candidate selection depends on the **full** layer's leaf set; isolated tests can't see candidates that only exist when the rest of the app is mounted.

The fix: a single test file that mounts `<App/>` and exercises every gesture — and which fails when any of these four regressions sneak back in.

### The shape of the test

#### File location

`kanban-app/ui/src/spatial-nav-end-to-end.spatial.test.tsx` (new file, top of `src/` because it is app-level, not component-level).

#### Mount the full App

```tsx
import App from "@/App";

await render(<App />);
```

`<App/>` already composes `SpatialFocusProvider → FocusLayer → CommandBusyProvider → RustEngineContainer → WindowContainer → AppModeContainer → BoardContainer → NavBar / ViewsContainer / PerspectivesContainer / PerspectiveContainer / ViewContainer / InspectorsContainer`. The test stubs no providers — it stubs only the **Tauri boundary** (`@tauri-apps/api/core` invoke, `@tauri-apps/api/event` listen, `@tauri-apps/api/window` getCurrent / WebviewWindow) so production data flows through `RustEngineContainer` from a fixture.

#### Tauri boundary stub

Three invokes need real responses for `<App/>` to bootstrap:

- `kanban_state_snapshot` (or whatever `RustEngineContainer` calls on mount) → returns the fixture (a board with 3 columns × 3 cards each, 2 perspectives, schema with `task.title`, `task.status`, `column.name`, `board.percent_complete`).
- `ui_state_snapshot` → returns `{ keymap_mode: "cua", windows: { main: { palette_open: false, palette_mode: "command", inspector_stack: [] } } }`.
- Any other bootstrap call the production code makes during `useEffect` mount — discover by booting the test against `mockInvoke = vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined)`, capturing the unhandled command names from `mockInvoke.mock.calls`, and adding a fixture response for each.

The remaining invokes — `spatial_register_zone`, `spatial_register_focusable`, `spatial_register_layer`, `spatial_focus`, `spatial_navigate`, `spatial_drill_in`, `spatial_drill_out`, `spatial_unregister_scope`, `spatial_update_rect` — are routed through the same shadow-registry + ported-navigator harness as `board-view.cross-column-nav.spatial.test.tsx`. **Reuse that harness** — extract its `vi.hoisted` setup and the ported `BeamNavStrategy` into a shared helper:

`kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-shadow-registry.ts` (new) — exports `setupSpatialHarness()` that returns `{ mockInvoke, fireFocusChanged, registry, getRegisteredKeyByMoniker(m) }`. Both the cross-column-nav test and this new end-to-end test import from it. No duplication.

#### Fixture

`kanban-app/ui/src/test/fixtures/end-to-end-board.ts` (new):

- Board `board:E2E` with `name = "End-to-End Test Board"`, `percent_complete = 50`.
- 3 columns: `column:TODO`, `column:DOING`, `column:DONE`.
- Each column has 3 task cards (`task:T1..T3`, `task:D1..D3`, `task:N1..N3`) with unique titles.
- 2 perspectives: `perspective:default` (active) and `perspective:secondary`, both with `view: "board"`.
- Schema with at least `task.title`, `task.status`, `column.name`, `board.percent_complete` field defs.

#### Gestures to walk

The test file contains one `describe` block per gesture family. Each test renders the app once at the top of the block (or per test if the gesture mutates state), drives the gesture, and asserts on observable DOM **and** the `mockInvoke` call log.

**Family 1 — Click → focus indicator visible.** For each of: a card, a column body whitespace, a perspective tab, a nav-bar button, a field row in the inspector:
- Click the element matched by `[data-moniker="<expected>"]`.
- Assert exactly one `mockInvoke("spatial_focus", { key })` call with the correct registered key (look up via `getRegisteredKeyByMoniker`).
- After `fireFocusChanged(key)`, assert the element carries `data-focused="true"` AND its descendant `[data-testid="focus-indicator"]` is mounted with the bar class signature (`pointer-events-none absolute -left-2 …`).
- Critically: assert exactly ONE `[data-focused="true"]` element exists in the document at any time — locks in the "single focus claim" invariant.

**Family 2 — hjkl / arrow navigation within and across zones.**
- Focus `task:T1`. Press `j` (vim) / `ArrowDown` (cua). Lands on `task:T2`.
- Press `j` again → `task:T3`.
- Press `l` / `ArrowRight` → lands on a card in `column:DOING` (the cross-column regression).
- Press `l` again → lands on a card in `column:DONE`.
- Press `h` / `ArrowLeft` → lands back in `column:DOING`.
- All four directions cycle correctly; no direction silently no-ops when a candidate exists.

**Family 3 — Drill in / Drill out.**
- Focus a column, press Enter → `mockInvoke("spatial_drill_in", { key: <column-key> })`. Subsequent `fireFocusChanged` drives focus to the column's first child leaf (`<column.name>` header or `task:T1`).
- Focus a card, press Escape → `mockInvoke("spatial_drill_out", …)`. Focus lands on the card's parent column.

**Family 4 — Space → inspect.**
- Focus a card, press Space. Asserts `mockInvoke` was called for `ui.inspect` with `target: "task:T1"`. Asserts the inspector mounts (the inspector container shows the task entity).
- Focus a perspective tab, press Space. Asserts NO `ui.inspect` dispatch — perspective is not an entity.

**Family 5 — Enter → rename on perspective tab.**
- Focus the active perspective tab, press Enter. Asserts `<InlineRenameEditor>` mounts (CodeMirror `.cm-editor` is a descendant of `[data-moniker^="perspective_tab:"]`).
- Type "RenameTest", press Enter inside the editor. Asserts `mockInvoke("perspective.rename", { args: { id, new_name: <original> + "RenameTest" } })`.
- Locks in `01KQ7GE3KY91X2YR6BX5AY40VK`.

**Family 6 — dblclick policy.**
- Double-click a card. Asserts `mockInvoke` for `ui.inspect` with `target: "task:T1"`.
- Double-click a perspective tab. Asserts `<InlineRenameEditor>` mounts AND zero `ui.inspect` calls. Locks in `01KQ7GM77B1E6YH8Z893K05VKY`.
- Double-click a nav-bar button background, the perspective bar background, the view container chrome. Each one: zero `ui.inspect` calls.

**Family 7 — Single focus indicator, no ring variant.**
- After mounting and focusing each leaf type, walk the document. Every `[data-testid="focus-indicator"]` carries the bar class signature; none carries `inset-0` or `ring-2`. Locks in `01KQ7G7SCN7ZQD4TFGP5EH4FFX`.

**Family 8 — Registry shape audit.** Walk the captured `mockInvoke` calls for `spatial_register_*`. Assert:
- `task:*` registered as `spatial_register_focusable` (leaves), never as `spatial_register_zone`.
- Each `task:*` carries `parent_zone === <its column's zone key>`.
- Each `column:*` carries `parent_zone === <ui:board's zone key>`.
- One `layer_key` per window. (If quick-capture pops a second window in the test, allow two; otherwise one.)
- Every `<Inspectable>`-wrapped entity (or `inspectOnDoubleClick={true}` if the wrapper refactor `01KQ7K7KZNR3EHS9SY0XY79NYE` hasn't landed yet) was the only path to `useDispatchCommand("ui.inspect")`.

**Family 9 — Edit-mode keystroke containment.** Focus a text input (e.g. inline rename editor or a card title editor). Type "abc". Assert ZERO `mockInvoke("spatial_navigate", …)` calls during the keystrokes — the input owns them. Locks in the editor-keystroke-isolation contract.

### Why this card depends on three others

- `01KQ7GWE9V2XKWYAQ0HCPDE0EZ` — until cross-column nav works, family 2 cannot pass.
- `01KQ7K7KZNR3EHS9SY0XY79NYE` — the `<Inspectable>` refactor changes which file owns the `ui.inspect` dispatch. Family 6's "no inspect dispatch on chrome" assertion is identical either way (boolean opt-in or wrapper composition), but Family 8's "single dispatch site" audit only fires post-refactor.
- `01KQ5PSMYE3Q60SV8270S6K819` — once `<Focusable>` is deleted, the registry-shape audit can drop the legacy primitive's name from its allowlist, simplifying Family 8.

If any of those three are still in flight when this card starts, the implementer either rebases or files small follow-ups; do not start this card before they're done.

### What this card does NOT do

- Does not introduce new app behavior. It only adds a test file and shared harness.
- Does not refactor the existing per-component spatial tests. They keep their narrow scope; this test is the umbrella.
- Does not run on every CI invocation as a fast unit — by design it is a slow browser-mode integration test. Add it to the existing `vitest run` browser project so it runs on PRs to `kanban-app/ui/`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/test/spatial-shadow-registry.ts` exports `setupSpatialHarness()` and is consumed by both this test and `board-view.cross-column-nav.spatial.test.tsx` (existing test refactored to import from the helper, no duplication).
- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/test/fixtures/end-to-end-board.ts` exists with the 3×3 board fixture, 2 perspectives, and minimal schema.
- [ ] The test mounts `<App/>` directly — not a partial component. Greppable: `import App from "@/App"` appears in the file.
- [ ] All nine families pass. Removing the fix from any of `01KQ7GM77B1E6YH8Z893K05VKY`, `01KQ7GE3KY91X2YR6BX5AY40VK`, `01KQ7GWE9V2XKWYAQ0HCPDE0EZ`, or `01KQ7G7SCN7ZQD4TFGP5EH4FFX` re-introduces the regression and a specific family fails — verified by `git revert <fix-commit>` locally and re-running.
- [ ] `cd kanban-app/ui && npm test` runs the file as part of the browser project (no separate test command, no opt-in flag).
- [ ] CI workflow `.github/workflows/*.yml` runs the test on every PR that touches `kanban-app/ui/`.

## Tests

This card IS the test. Its own pass criterion is that the new file's nine families all run green against the post-prerequisites tree. No additional test files are required.

### How to run

```
cd kanban-app/ui && npm test -- spatial-nav-end-to-end
```

(Or unfiltered: `cd kanban-app/ui && npm test`.) Headless on CI.

  1. Extract `setupSpatialHarness()` from the existing `board-view.cross-column-nav.spatial.test.tsx` into the shared helper — confirm that test still passes.

  3. Write the failing Family 1 (click → focus indicator) — confirms the harness mounts `<App/>` correctly.
  4. Add families 2–9 one at a time, each one green before moving on.
  5. Run `git revert` on each of the four prerequisite-fix commits in turn (locally, do not push) and confirm the matching family fails. Restore.

-### Architecturally significant decisions
+- Does not introduce new app behavior. It only adds a test file and shared harness.
+- Does not refactor the existing per-component spatial tests. They keep their narrow scope; this test is the umbrella.
+- Does not run on every CI invocation as a fast unit — by design it is a slow browser-mode integration test. Add it to the existing `vitest run` browser project so it runs on PRs to `kanban-app/ui/`.

-- **`vi.mock` is file-scoped — the helper does not declare them.** Vitest hoists `vi.mock(path, factory)` to the top of the FILE the call appears in, and from there it intercepts that file's transitive imports. A `vi.mock` in the helper module would not apply when a test file's `import App from "@/App"` cascades into modules like `views-context.tsx` that call `getCurrentWindow()` at module-load time. The pattern that satisfies vitest's hoisting AND keeps the helper as the single owner of the mock spy state is: each consumer test file declares its own `vi.mock` calls and forwards to the helper's exported spies via a `vi.hoisted` factory that dynamic-imports the helper. The helper's docstring documents this pattern as the canonical recipe.
-- **Bootstrap-invoke handler discovers commands lazily.** The fixture's `bootstrapInvokeImpl` returns the right shape for every Tauri command the production provider stack fires on mount: `list_entity_types`, `get_entity_schema`, `get_board_data`, `list_entities` (per type), `list_open_boards`, `list_views`, `get_ui_state`, `get_undo_state`, `dispatch_command` (with per-cmd routing for `perspective.list`, `perspective.set`, `perspective.save`, `perspective.rename`, `view.set`, `ui.inspect`, `ui.setFocus`, `file.switchBoard`), and `list_commands_for_scope`. Unknown commands return `undefined` — the Tauri default for void-result commands — so the App degrades gracefully instead of throwing.
-- **Tailwind-substitute stylesheet.** The browser test project does not load `@tailwindcss/vite` (production-build-only). Without it, `className="flex flex-1 …"` collapses to no-op. The end-to-end test injects the same small Tailwind substitute the cross-column-nav test uses (extended for `h-screen` / `flex-row` / `h-12` / etc.) so cross-column geometry is faithful.
-- **`spatial_push_layer` not `spatial_register_layer`.** The Family 8 layer-count assertion was originally written against `spatial_register_layer`, but the production code calls `spatial_push_layer` (see `lib/spatial-focus-context.tsx`). Updated to filter on the correct command name.
